preview_text:
The model rates Turkey as the slight favorite at 37.5% to win, while the USA holds a 31.1% chance of victory. A draw appears nearly as probable as either outcome under the model's estimate, sitting at 31.4%.

The three outcomes are tightly bunched, suggesting a competitive match with genuine uncertainty across all results. Turkey carries only a 6-percentage-point edge over the USA in win probability, and the 31% draw probability indicates the teams are sufficiently matched that a stalemate is a plausible and substantial outcome. The match is expected to be closely contested rather than tilted decisively toward either side.

The market consensus diverges meaningfully from the model. Sportsbooks and Polymarket both favor the USA decisively—around 49–50% win probability—while assessing Turkey's chances at roughly 27%. The market also compresses the draw to 22–24%, well below the model's 31%. This represents roughly an 18-percentage-point gap on the USA and a 10-percentage-point gap on Turkey. The dashboard does not adjudicate which source is more accurate.
